title: Kainchi Dham
tagline: Kainchi Dham — spiritual town of Uttarakhand

overview.short: Kainchi Dham also known as Neem Karori Ashram is a spiritual ashram and Hindu temple complex, located at Kainchi in the Nainital district, Kumaon region of Uttarakhand, India.
overview.description: Kainchi Dham also known as Neem Karori Ashram is a spiritual ashram and Hindu temple complex, located at Kainchi in the Nainital district, Kumaon region of Uttarakhand, India. It is known for its association with Neem Karoli Baba, a revered Hindu saint. The ashram was established by Neem Karoli Baba in 1964 on land donated by a local resident, Purnanand. Kainchi Dham is situated at a distance of 9 km from Bhowali and 17 km from Nainital, on the Nainital–Almora Road.

topPlaces.name: Ramgarh, Uttarakhand
topPlaces.description: For other places with the same name, see Ramgarh Ramgarh is a hill station and tourist destination in Nainital district of Uttarakhand, India. It is situated 30 km from Nainital, between to Mukteshwar and Bhimtal.

topPlaces.name: Bhowali
topPlaces.description: Bhowali (Kumaoni: Bhoāli)[bʱəʋaːli] is a town and municipal board in Nainital District in the state of Uttarakhand, India. It is situated at a distance of 11 km (6.8 mi) from the city of Nainital, the district headquarters; at an average elevation of 1,654 m (5,427 ft) from sea level.

topPlaces.name: Ghorakhal
topPlaces.description: Ghorakal is the place situated in the Nainital district of the Uttarakhand state of India Ghorakhal means pond for water to horses. It is the picturesque site at the height of more than 2,000 m.

topPlaces.name: Birla Vidya Mandir
topPlaces.description: Birla Vidya Mandir in Nainital is a residential boarding school for boys in India which was founded on 17 July 1947 just before Indian Independence. The school is mainly the product of the vision of Govind Ballabh Pant, an Indian independence activist.

topPlaces.name: G. B. Pant High Altitude Zoo
topPlaces.description: Pt. Govind Ballabh Pant High Altitude Zoo is a high altitude zoo situated in Nainital, Kumaon, Uttarakhand, India.

image.alt: G. B. Pant High Altitude Zoo

topPlaces.name: Nainital Cantonment
topPlaces.description: Nainital Cantonment is a cantonment town in Nainital district in the Indian state of Uttarakhand, close to the hill station of Nainital. Established in the year 1878, today the Nainital Cantonment is a Class IV cantonment.

topPlaces.name: Naini Tal Yacht Club
topPlaces.description: Naini Tal Yacht Club is a yacht club based on Nainital Lake, India. At an elevation of 2,084 m (6,837 ft), it is the highest yacht club in India and amongst the highest in the world.

topPlaces.name: Nainital
topPlaces.description: Nainital (Kumaoni: Naintāl; pronounced [nɛnːtaːl]), formerly anglicized as Naini Tal, is a town and headquarters of Nainital district of Kumaon division, Uttarakhand, India. It is the judicial capital of Uttarakhand, the High Court of the state being located there and is the headquarters of an eponymous district.

items.note: Pt. Govind Ballabh Pant High Altitude Zoo is a high altitude zoo situated in Nainital, Kumaon, Uttarakhand, India.

Nainital Cantonment is a cantonment town in Nainital district in the Indian state of Uttarakhand, close to the hill station of Nainital. Established in the year 1878, today the Nainital Cantonment is a Class IV cantonment.

itinerary.title: Day 3 — Naini Tal Yacht Club & around

items.note: Naini Tal Yacht Club is a yacht club based on Nainital Lake, India. At an elevation of 2,084 m (6,837 ft), it is the highest yacht club in India and amongst the highest in the world.

Nainital (Kumaoni: Naintāl; pronounced [nɛnːtaːl]), formerly anglicized as Naini Tal, is a town and headquarters of Nainital district of Kumaon division, Uttarakhand, India. It is the judicial capital of Uttarakhand, the High Court of the state being located there and is the headquarters of an eponymous district.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Kainchi Dham?
faq.a: The best time to visit Kainchi Dham is Mar – Jun, Sep – Nov. Summers are 15–28°C and winters are 2–16°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Kainchi Dham?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Pantnagar Airport (43 km) and the nearest railway station is Kathgodam (Nainital railhead) (17 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Kainchi Dham?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Kainchi Dham at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Kainchi Dham?
faq.a: Stays in Kainchi Dham start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Kainchi Dham?
faq.a: The top attractions are Ramgarh, Uttarakhand, Bhowali, Ghorakhal, Birla Vidya Mandir, G. B. Pant High Altitude Zoo.
